analysis: Seven nights in a row above six and a half hours, with four of them clearing seven and a half — that's not luck, that's the stack working. Your best night was 7h 56m with only 32 minutes awake, and your REM has been genuinely gorgeous all week, hitting two hours on two separate nights and barely dipping below 1h 38m on any of them. The thing to keep an eye on is deep sleep, which has been a little shy — mostly in the high twenties and thirties of minutes, with only that one 53-minute spike on the second night — and awake time crept up to over an hour on two nights, including 1h 17m on the tenth. None of that cancels the momentum, it just means the wind-down ritual is doing the heavy lifting on the front end, and something's still nudging you awake in the back half of the night.
